title: The Hormuz Reopening Trade: These 20 Large-Cap Stocks Still Haven't Caught Up To Pre-War Levels
author: Piero Cingari
description: Following President Trump's announcement of a U.S.-Iran peace deal and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, oil prices plunged 5.4% to $80/barrel. However, 20 large-cap stocks worth over $100 billion remain trading 15-24% below their pre-war levels from February 27, 2026. The laggards span consumer staples, healthcare, software, and mining sectors, with weakness extending beyond the war premium as these companies face ongoing margin pressures from higher energy costs.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Down 24% from pre-war levels, the worst performer on the screen, indicating significant underperformance despite market rally
Trading below February 27 pre-war close by 15-24%, consumer staple facing margin pressures from elevated energy costs
Among the lagging consumer anchors still trading below pre-war levels despite Hormuz reopening
Consumer staple anchor trading below pre-war levels, affected by ongoing cost pressures
China's largest internet company hit hardest by higher energy costs as China is world's biggest crude importer; among worst performers
Chinese internet name near bottom of laggards, impacted by elevated energy costs affecting Chinese companies
Software sector laggard trading below pre-war levels despite market rally
Software and IT services company among the screened laggards below pre-war close
IT services company screening among laggards, trading below pre-war levels
Mining sector laggard trading below pre-war levels despite gold trade benefiting from geopolitical uncertainty

title: Payhawk’s Summer ’26 Edition delivers enterprise-grade control to finance teams without enterprise complexity
author: Not Specified
description: Payhawk announced its Summer '26 Edition, a major platform update featuring native SAP S/4HANA integration, role-based field visibility, expanded global payments in new currencies (CHF, DKK, PLN), unified invoice capture, and enhanced travel flexibility. The update aims to help finance teams manage complex operations with intuitive workflows and reduced manual overhead.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: SAP is mentioned as a technology partner through native integration with SAP S/4HANA and support for NetSuite and Microsoft Dynamics. While the integration is presented as beneficial, SAP itself is not the subject of the announcement and no direct impact on SAP's business is implied.

Microsoft is mentioned for Dynamics ERP integration and Teams integration for AI-native workflows. These are presented as positive integrations for Payhawk users, but Microsoft is not the focus of the announcement and no direct business impact is indicated.

title: Asset Management Market Surges to $18,071 Billion by 2035 as AI, IoT, and Predictive Maintenance Transform Enterprise Operations | Research by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global Asset Management Market is projected to grow from $885.92 billion in 2025 to $18,071 billion by 2035, with a CAGR of 35.2%. Growth is driven by AI and IoT integration enabling predictive maintenance, with software dominating at 64% market share and cloud-based deployment leading. North America holds 39% market share, while the U.S. market is expected to grow from $96.69 billion to $677.89 billion by 2035.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: IBM Maximo platform is highlighted as a leading enterprise asset management solution managing physical equipment across manufacturing, energy, and infrastructure sectors, positioning the company to benefit from the 35.2% CAGR market growth.
Oracle EAM platform is mentioned as a major player in enterprise asset management, well-positioned to capture growth from the expanding market driven by AI and IoT adoption.
Oracle EAM platform is mentioned as a major player in enterprise asset management, well-positioned to capture growth from the expanding market driven by AI and IoT adoption.

SAP PM platform is identified as a key enterprise asset management solution, positioned to benefit from the market's strong growth trajectory and increasing demand for predictive maintenance capabilities.
Listed among key players in the asset management market, positioned to benefit from cloud-based deployment dominance and digital transformation trends driving market expansion.
Identified as a key player in asset management solutions, well-positioned to capitalize on growth in manufacturing and industrial sectors driving the market's 35.2% CAGR.

Recent 2025 launch of ABB Ability SmartMaster for predictive maintenance demonstrates active innovation and market engagement, positioning the company to benefit from the growing adoption of AI-driven asset management solutions.

title: Procurement Analytics Market Worth $50.13 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global procurement analytics market, valued at $6.12 billion in 2025, is projected to reach $50.13 billion by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 23.49%. The U.S. market is expected to grow from $2.01 billion to $15.86 billion, while Europe is projected to reach $13.42 billion. Growth is driven by enterprise digitalization, cloud-based platforms, ESG compliance requirements, and adoption of AI-driven predictive analytics for supplier management.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: SAP expanded AI-powered sourcing and supplier risk analytics capabilities within SAP Ariba in 2026, demonstrating active innovation and investment in procurement analytics solutions to improve automation and enterprise spend visibility.

Oracle enhanced predictive procurement analytics and supplier intelligence tools across Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications in 2026, showing commitment to strengthening real-time sourcing optimization and procurement decision-making capabilities.

Oracle enhanced predictive procurement analytics and supplier intelligence tools across Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications in 2026, showing commitment to strengthening real-time sourcing optimization and procurement decision-making capabilities.
Identified as a key player in the procurement analytics market, well-positioned to capitalize on growing enterprise demand for AI-driven procurement intelligence and digital transformation solutions.

title: Insurance Analytics Market Size to Hit USD 72.39 Billion by 2035 | Research by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global Insurance Analytics Market is valued at $17.60 billion in 2025 and expected to reach $72.39 billion by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 15.19%. Growth is driven by fraud detection, AI adoption, cloud computing, and alternative data sources like telematics and IoT sensors. Software components and cloud deployment dominate the market, while SMEs and government agencies represent the fastest-growing segments.
